Certificate path is required for Manual: Certificate path is required for Manual mode. Upload a certificate file or enter its path.
Changes take effect on the next API requ: Changes take effect on the next API request — no restart needed.
Changes take effect on the next service: Changes take effect on the next service restart.
Choose a data provider for each service:
Choose a data provider for each service domain.
       Providers that match your station location are pre-selected.
       For providers that require an API key, the key fields appear inline when you select them.
Choose how values are displayed on your:
Choose how values are displayed on your weather dashboard.
       Use the preset buttons to fill all fields at once, or adjust individual groups below.
Choose meters or feet for the altitude v: Choose meters or feet for the altitude value.
Choose the display unit for this measure: Choose the display unit for this measurement.
Clear Skies Operator License Agreement t: Clear Skies Operator License Agreement text
Collection runs independently — pairs ar: Collection runs independently — pairs are stored even when correction is disabled.
Column mapping — database column to cano: Column mapping — database column to canonical weewx field
Configuration directory not set — cannot: Configuration directory not set — cannot save uploaded files.
Configure a live webcam image and timela: Configure a live webcam image and timelapse video for your dashboard.
Configure analytics tracking, visitor re: Configure analytics tracking, visitor region disclosures, and optional legal content overrides.
Configure optional feature-specific sett: Configure optional feature-specific settings for your weather site.
Confirm your weather station's location:
Confirm your weather station's location and display name.
       These are used for provider geo-routing and the dashboard header.
Controls the consent banner shown to vis: Controls the consent banner shown to visitors based on their geographic region.
Controls the initial theme shown to visi: Controls the initial theme shown to visitors. Auto (OS) follows the visitor's system preference.
Could not connect to the API. Check the: Could not connect to the API. Check the address and try again.
Could not fetch database defaults from t: Could not fetch database defaults from the API. Enter the settings below.
Could not fetch station details from the: Could not fetch station details from the API. Fill in the fields below manually.
Could not fetch the database schema from: Could not fetch the database schema from the API — check your connection settings in step 2 and try again.
Could not find skin.conf in /etc/weewx/s: Could not find skin.conf in /etc/weewx/skins/{skin_name}/. Check the skin name.
Could not reach the API to apply configu: Could not reach the API to apply configuration. Check that the API is running and try again.
Could not reach the API to fetch databas: Could not reach the API to fetch database defaults. Enter the settings below.
Could not reach the API to fetch station: Could not reach the API to fetch station details. Fill in the fields below manually.
Could not reach the API to fetch the dat: Could not reach the API to fetch the database schema. Check that the API is running and try again.
Could not reach the API to test the conn: Could not reach the API to test the connection. Check that the API is running and try again.
Could not reach the API. Check the addre: Could not reach the API. Check the address and try again.
Could not read the database schema — che: Could not read the database schema — check your connection settings in step 2 and try again.
Could not read the uploaded file: {error: Could not read the uploaded file: {error}
Could not verify the API connection: {de: Could not verify the API connection: {detail}
Current Terms of Use content preview: Current Terms of Use content preview
Custom columns requiring canonical name: Custom columns requiring canonical name assignment
Customize your site's identity, logos, c: Customize your site's identity, logos, colors, and theme.
DNS provider API token is required.
DNS provider is required for DNS-01 chal: DNS provider is required for DNS-01 challenge.
DNS provider whose API will create the v: DNS provider whose API will create the validation record for DNS-01 verification.
Davis VP2 pyranometers: ±3–5%.\n Ambi:
Davis VP2 pyranometers: ±3–5%.
    Ambient Weather stations: approximately ±15%.
    If you use consumer-grade equipment, tight threshold values may produce
    unreliable classifications during marginal sky conditions.
    Consider relaxing the thresholds by 0.05–0.10 for less precise sensors.
Decimal degrees. North is positive (e.g. 38.8894); South is negative (e.g. -33.8688).
Decimal degrees. West is negative (e.g. -77.0352); East is positive (e.g. 151.2093).
Default: enabled. When disabled, haze co:
Default: enabled. When disabled, haze conditions will not be reported
        regardless of PM2.5 levels.
Deletes all per-month baselines from the:
Deletes all per-month baselines from the API. The system will re-bootstrap
      as new clear-sky samples are collected. This cannot be undone.
Describes the logo for screen readers. R: Describes the logo for screen readers. Required when a logo is uploaded.
Displayed in the browser tab and dashboa: Displayed in the browser tab and dashboard header.
Domain name is required for automated TL: Domain name is required for automated TLS.
Downloading… this may take a few minutes: Downloading… this may take a few minutes.
Downloads OpenStreetMap geographic featu:
Downloads OpenStreetMap geographic features (boundaries, roads, water) for the
    satellite map overlay. The data is stored as a PMTiles file on the API host and
    served to the dashboard on demand. Downloads may take several minutes depending
    on the station location and network speed.
Drag cards between the zones to build yo:
Drag cards between the zones to build your Now page layout, or use the
    keyboard buttons. Changes take effect when you click
Drag-and-drop card arrangement for the N: Drag-and-drop card arrangement for the Now page.
Edit component settings. Each section is: Edit component settings. Each section is independently saved. Changes are merged into the managed region of the relevant config file — your free-form customisations below the managed region are not touched.
Edit the file directly or re-run the set: Edit the file directly or re-run the setup wizard to change them.
Email address for Let's Encrypt certific: Email address for Let's Encrypt certificate expiry notifications.
Enable only after a model has been train: Enable only after a model has been trained.
Enter a new value to update: Enter a new value to update
Enter a skin name or upload a file, or c: Enter a skin name or upload a file, or click Skip — Start Fresh.
Enter a weewx unit name, e.g. degree_F,: Enter a weewx unit name, e.g. degree_F, km_per_hour, mm.
Enter credentials to test connectivity. Keys are stored in
Enter key to test (not saved here): Enter key to test (not saved here)
Enter new value to update, or leave blan: Enter new value to update, or leave blank to keep existing
Enter the connection details shown in yo: Enter the connection details shown in your API's startup banner.
Enter the key to run a connectivity test: Enter the key to run a connectivity test below. To permanently update this key use the setup wizard.
Enter the path to your weewx SQLite data:
Enter the path to your weewx SQLite database file.
       The path is pre-filled from the API's weewx.conf where available.
Enter your weewx MariaDB/MySQL connectio:
Enter your weewx MariaDB/MySQL connection details.
       Fields are pre-filled from the API's weewx.conf where available.
Filesystem path to your TLS certificate: Filesystem path to your TLS certificate file (PEM format).
Filesystem path to your TLS private key: Filesystem path to your TLS private key file (PEM format).
For full deployment instructions includi:
For full deployment instructions including Docker Compose, reverse proxy examples,
      and TLS setup, see the
For self-hosted instances with BBOX crop:
For self-hosted instances with BBOX cropping, enter the bounding box
               so the dashboard limits zoom-out to your coverage area. Leave empty for
               global coverage (public API).
For self-hosted instances with BBOX cropping, enter the bounding box so the dashboard limits zoom-out to your coverage area. Leave empty for global coverage (public API).
Four comma-separated decimal values. Exa: Four comma-separated decimal values. Example for SoCal: 32.0,-120.5,35.5,-114.5
Full URL including scheme and port. Use:
Full URL including scheme and port. Use a hostname (FQDN) rather than
        an IP address for dual-stack compatibility.
        Example:
Full URL to your Facebook page or profil: Full URL to your Facebook page or profile.
Full URL to your Instagram profile.
Full URL to your LibreWxR instance. Must: Full URL to your LibreWxR instance. Must be reachable by Caddy.
Full URL to your Twitter / X profile.
Full URL to your YouTube channel.
Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID (start: Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID (starts with G-). Leave blank to disable tracking entirely.
Hostname or IP address of the MariaDB/My: Hostname or IP address of the MariaDB/MySQL server. Use
How Caddy handles HTTPS certificates. Se: How Caddy handles HTTPS certificates. Self-signed for development; ACME for automatic Let's Encrypt; Manual for your own certs; Behind Proxy when TLS is handled upstream.
How often (in seconds) the dashboard re-: How often (in seconds) the dashboard re-fetches the webcam snapshot. Lower values show more current images.
How should Clear Skies handle browser-fa: How should Clear Skies handle browser-facing HTTPS? This configures the Caddy reverse proxy's TLS certificate.
How your weather site identifies itself: How your weather site identifies itself to visitors.
Hygroscopic correction exponent (Hänel 1:
Hygroscopic correction exponent (Hänel 1976). Default 0.45 is suitable
        for most locations. Lower values reduce humidity sensitivity; higher values
        increase it.
I have read and agree to the Clear Skies:
I have read and agree to the Clear Skies Operator License Agreement
          (EULA Version 2.0, Last Updated 2026-07-02).
ICO or PNG, max 100 KB. Replaces the URL: ICO or PNG, max 100 KB. Replaces the URL below when a file is chosen.
IP address or hostname of the server run: IP address or hostname of the server running the Clear Skies API.
IQAir returns both US EPA and China MEP: IQAir returns both US EPA and China MEP indices.
If the dashboard does not load or shows:
If the dashboard does not load or shows stale data after a minute or two,
      you can restart the services manually on your weewx host:
If you enter coordinates above, the time: If you enter coordinates above, the timezone will be auto-detected.
If you have a Belchertown graphs.conf fi: If you have a Belchertown graphs.conf file, upload it to automatically convert your chart layout to Clear Skies format. This is optional — you can configure charts manually later.
If you have an existing weewx skin (like:
If you have an existing weewx skin (like Belchertown), enter its name to import
       the configuration and pre-fill the steps that follow.
       Otherwise, click
Initial time window shown when visitors: Initial time window shown when visitors open the Seismic page. Visitors can change this.
Invalid trust token. Check the token pri: Invalid trust token. Check the token printed in the API terminal and try again.
Kasten-Czeplak clearness index to sky co: Kasten-Czeplak clearness index to sky cover mapping
Key path is required for Manual mode. Up: Key path is required for Manual mode. Upload a key file or enter its path.
Km threshold between Clear and Few Cloud: Km threshold between Clear and Few Clouds. Above this value, sky is classified as Clear.
Km threshold between Few Clouds and Scat: Km threshold between Few Clouds and Scattered. Above this value (and below Few Max), sky is Few Clouds.
Km threshold between Scattered and Broke: Km threshold between Scattered and Broken. Above this value (and below Scattered Max), sky is Scattered.
Km threshold for Overcast classification: Km threshold for Overcast classification based on the clearness index.
Kv variability threshold for Overcast cl: Kv variability threshold for Overcast classification.
Leave a field blank to remove the mappin: Leave a field blank to remove the mapping.
Leave blank to keep the current value.
Leave blank to keep the existing passwor: Leave blank to keep the existing password. Stored securely in secrets.env.
LibreWxR provides high-quality radar (zo:
LibreWxR provides high-quality radar (zoom 12), 13 color schemes,
             60-minute nowcast, and weather alerts. Use the public API for zero-setup,
             or point to your own self-hosted instance for production reliability.

LibreWxR provides high-quality radar (zoom 12), 13 color schemes, 60-minute nowcast, and weather alerts. Use the public API for zero-setup, or point to your own self-hosted instance for production reliability.
Logo displayed when the visitor uses a d: Logo displayed when the visitor uses a dark colour scheme. PNG or SVG, max 500 KB.
Logo displayed when the visitor uses a l: Logo displayed when the visitor uses a light colour scheme. PNG or SVG, max 500 KB.
Manage your Clear Skies configuration. C: Manage your Clear Skies configuration. Click
Map database column names to canonical w: Map database column names to canonical weewx field names.
Map database columns to weewx canonical: Map database columns to weewx canonical field names.
MariaDB/MySQL username with read access: MariaDB/MySQL username with read access to the weewx database.
Minimum sun elevation (degrees above hor: Minimum sun elevation (degrees above horizon) for sky classification to operate. Below this, conditions default to the provider forecast.
Name: Name
Name of the weewx archive database. The: Name of the weewx archive database. The default is usually correct.
No cards in the active layout yet. Drag:
No cards in the active layout yet. Drag from the palette below or

No reference sensors found within 25 km.
No schema data available. Check your dat: No schema data available. Check your database connection in step 2.
No sensor selected yet. The system will:
No sensor selected yet. The system will select a reference sensor automatically
        on the next bootstrap run.
No values configured — run the setup wiz: No values configured — run the setup wizard first.
No values found for this section. This s: No values found for this section. This section may not have been configured during setup. Save with new values to create it.
Non-reference sensors (PurpleAir, privat:
Non-reference sensors (PurpleAir, private) are accepted here but not
              listed in the dropdown above.
Non-secret values are written into the m: Non-secret values are written into the managed region of the config file.
Not connected — go back to step 1: Not connected — go back to step 1
Only one download runs at a time. If a d:
Only one download runs at a time. If a download is already in progress,
        this button will wait for it to complete before returning.
Only show earthquakes at or above this m: Only show earthquakes at or above this magnitude. The Richter scale is logarithmic — each whole number is roughly 32× more energy.
Open-Meteo computes both US and European: Open-Meteo computes both US and European AQI server-side.
OpenAQ provides PM2.5 and PM10 from gove:
OpenAQ provides PM2.5 and PM10 from government reference monitors
     in 141 countries. Observed data — eligible for haze confirmation.
     Data lag is approximately 1–2 hours; Aeris or IQAir are preferred
     when available. No regional scale configuration is required.
OpenStreetMap boundaries, roads, and wat: OpenStreetMap boundaries, roads, and water for the satellite map overlay.
OpenWeatherMap uses its own 1–5 air qual:
OpenWeatherMap uses its own 1–5 air quality scale.
     No regional configuration is required.
Optional. JPG, PNG, or WebP, max 2 MB. D: Optional. JPG, PNG, or WebP, max 2 MB. Displayed on the About page.
Optional. Leave blank if not used. When: Optional. Leave blank if not used. When provided, social media links appear in the dashboard footer.
Optional. Upload your own Terms of Use a: Optional. Upload your own Terms of Use and Privacy Policy to replace the default content on your Legal page. Leave blank to use the built-in templates.
Or upload a skin.conf file manually: Or upload a skin.conf file manually
PEM-encoded certificate, max 100 KB. Rep: PEM-encoded certificate, max 100 KB. Replaces the path above when a file is chosen.
PEM-encoded private key, max 100 KB. Sto: PEM-encoded private key, max 100 KB. Stored securely on the server, never web-served. Replaces the path above when a file is chosen.
PNG or SVG, max 500 KB. Replaces the URL: PNG or SVG, max 500 KB. Replaces the URL below when a file is chosen.
Path to the SQLite database file. This s: Path to the SQLite database file. This should match the path configured in your weewx.conf. The default weewx SQLite database is usually at /var/lib/weewx/weewx.sdb.
Phase 4: takes effect after the API upda: Phase 4: takes effect after the API update.
Please select a TLS configuration mode.
Primary highlight colour used for button: Primary highlight colour used for buttons, links, and active states across the dashboard.
Provides current air quality index readi: Provides current air quality index readings.
Provides multi-day weather forecasts sho: Provides multi-day weather forecasts shown on the dashboard.
Provides radar imagery for the dashboard: Provides radar imagery for the dashboard map tile.
Provides recent earthquake activity near: Provides recent earthquake activity near your station.
Provides severe weather warnings and wat: Provides severe weather warnings and watches for your area.
Re-run failed: the proxy secret is not i: Re-run failed: the proxy secret is not in secrets.env. If you deleted secrets.env, re-run the installer to regenerate it, or restart the API with --reset to start a fresh setup.
Read the Clear Skies Operator License Ag:
Read the Clear Skies Operator License Agreement (EULA) below, then check the
       box to confirm you have read and agree to its terms.
Redirecting to your dashboard in {n} sec: Redirecting to your dashboard in {n} seconds...
Required for accessibility when a photo: Required for accessibility when a photo is uploaded. Describe what is shown in the image (e.g. "Rooftop weather station with anemometer and rain gauge").
Reset all haze calibration data — this c: Reset all haze calibration data — this cannot be undone
Reset restores all thresholds to the fac: Reset restores all thresholds to the factory defaults listed above.
Review the text below and check the box: Review the text below and check the box again to continue.
Review your archive table schema. Stock:
Review your archive table schema. Stock weewx columns are auto-mapped.
       Assign canonical names to any custom columns, or leave them blank to exclude from API.
Review your configuration before applyin:
Review your configuration before applying.
       Applying will send the API configuration to the remote API, then write
SHA-256 fingerprint shown in the API sta:
SHA-256 fingerprint shown in the API startup banner. Used to verify the server's
        identity before sending the trust token.
Search radius in kilometres from your st: Search radius in kilometres from your station. Earthquakes beyond this distance are not shown on the Seismic page.
Select a canonical name from the dropdow:
Select a canonical name from the dropdown, or leave blank to exclude from API.
            Columns with a suggested match are pre-selected — verify and adjust as needed.
Select a section from the sidebar to vie: Select a section from the sidebar to view and edit its settings.
Select a sensor to populate the ID field: Select a sensor to populate the ID field below, then click “Set sensor override.” Only reference-grade (AQMD/regulatory) monitors are listed.
Select pages to hide from navigation. \": Select pages to hide from navigation. "Now" is always visible.
Select pages to hide from the dashboard: Select pages to hide from the dashboard navigation. The Now page is always visible and cannot be hidden.
Select the language for the setup wizard: Select the language for the setup wizard.
Selects the regional AQI standard return: Selects the regional AQI standard returned by Aeris.
Show auto-detected and heuristic unit su: Show auto-detected and heuristic unit suggestions pre-filled where available.
Show empty fields for manual unit entry.
Show the Webcam page on the dashboard. W: Show the Webcam page on the dashboard. When disabled, the page is hidden from navigation.
Shown in the dashboard footer (e.g. your: Shown in the dashboard footer (e.g. your name or organisation).
Shown in the dashboard header. Leave bla: Shown in the dashboard header. Leave blank to use the default.
Shown on the About page. Supports plain: Shown on the About page. Supports plain text. Leave empty to skip.
Shown when the visitor uses a dark colou: Shown when the visitor uses a dark colour scheme. PNG or SVG, max 500 KB.
Shown when the visitor uses a light colo: Shown when the visitor uses a light colour scheme. PNG or SVG, max 500 KB.
Single-use token printed when the API fi:
Single-use token printed when the API first starts. It cannot be re-used — if you
        need a new token, restart the API with
Small icon shown in the browser tab. ICO: Small icon shown in the browser tab. ICO or PNG, max 100 KB. Leave blank to use the default Clear Skies icon.
Small icon shown in the browser tab. Lea: Small icon shown in the browser tab. Leave blank for the default Clear Skies icon.
Start with sensible US-unit defaults. Yo: Start with sensible US-unit defaults. You can adjust all settings in the steps that follow.
Stock columns automatically mapped to ca: Stock columns automatically mapped to canonical names
Temperature bias correction using foreca: Temperature bias correction using forecast-observation pairs and Random Forest regression.
The API URL is where the reverse proxy (: The API URL is where the reverse proxy (Caddy) routes
The API bind address is set in: The API bind address is set in
The API did not accept the proxy secret. Check that secrets.env contains the correct WEEWX_CLEARSKIES_PROXY_SECRET and that the API is running with the same value.
The API is already configured. Verify th: The API is already configured. Verify the address below and click
The API is temporarily unavailable. Wait: The API is temporarily unavailable. Wait a moment and try again.
The API is unreachable or not yet config:
The API is unreachable or not yet configured. Start the API service and
        reload this page to view calibration status.

The API is unreachable or not yet configured. Start the API service and
        reload this page to view forecast correction status.

The API is unreachable or not yet configured. Start the API service and
        reload this page to view geographic features status.
The API returned an error ({status_code}: The API returned an error ({status_code}). Check the API server log and try again.
The API service did not come back online: The API service did not come back online within 5 minutes. It may need attention on the server.
The API will apply the new mapping on th: The API will apply the new mapping on the next request — no service restart needed.
The configuration directory has not been: The configuration directory has not been set. Please restart the setup tool with the correct --config-dir option.
The database password and provider API k:
The database password and provider API keys are stored in the API's own secrets file on the weewx host.
      Do not commit any secrets file to version control.
The default display language for the das: The default display language for the dashboard. Operators and visitors can override this in the UI.
The domain name for your weather dashboa: The domain name for your weather dashboard (e.g. weather.example.com). Required for ACME certificate issuance.
The forecast correction engine learns sy:
The forecast correction engine learns systematic temperature bias patterns
     at your station using Random Forest regression. It collects forecast-observation
     pairs, trains a model, and applies corrections to hourly forecast temperatures
     before serving them.
The haze detection calibration bootstrap:
The haze detection calibration bootstrap pulls historical PM2.5 data
           from
The haze detection system uses particula:
The haze detection system uses particulate matter (PM2.5) data and a
     hygroscopic scattering model to identify haze conditions. Calibration
     collects clear-sky solar transmittance samples month-by-month to establish
     a local seasonal baseline.
The proxy secret was accepted and a re-r: The proxy secret was accepted and a re-run session is open.
The sky cover classifier uses the Kasten:
The sky cover classifier uses the Kasten-Czeplak clearness index (K<sub>m</sub>) —
     the ratio of measured solar irradiance to the clear-sky theoretical maximum.
     Adjust the thresholds below to match your sensor's characteristics.
The trust token has been exchanged and a: The trust token has been exchanged and a setup session is open.
They contain the proxy shared secret: They contain the proxy shared secret
This API has already been set up. If you: This API has already been set up. If you need to reconfigure it, restart the API with the --reset flag.
This API was configured previously. The:
This API was configured previously. The trust token is not required.
    Confirm the address and port, then click
This provider is not available. Please g: This provider is not available. Please go back and choose a different provider.
This value is also saved in: This value is also saved in
This will delete all calibration data an: This will delete all calibration data and require re-bootstrap. Continue?
This will retrain the correction model. Training typically takes a few seconds.
Triggers a fresh download of OpenStreetM:
Triggers a fresh download of OpenStreetMap geographic features clipped to
      the station's configured bounding box. The download runs synchronously —
      the page will wait until the download is complete (typically 1–5 minutes).
Type a weewx field name or choose from t: Type a weewx field name or choose from the autocomplete list.
URL of the current webcam snapshot image: URL of the current webcam snapshot image, refreshed by your webcam software.
URL of the timelapse video file.
URL to a custom CSS file loaded after de: URL to a custom CSS file loaded after default styles. Use for advanced visual overrides.
Upload Privacy Policy (Markdown or text: Upload Privacy Policy (Markdown or text file)
Upload Terms of Use (Markdown or text fi: Upload Terms of Use (Markdown or text file)
Upload a .md or .txt file (max 100 KB). Markdown files render as-is. Plain text files are auto-formatted with heading structure.
Use browser location to fill latitude an: Use browser location to fill latitude and longitude
Use this if the API cannot reach the ski:
Use this if the API cannot reach the skin directory directly.
          Select the
Used for pressure calculations. Enter 0: Used for pressure calculations. Enter 0 if unknown.
Uses machine learning to enhance tempera: Uses machine learning to enhance temperature and wind speed predictions. Recommended for most operators.
Would you like mapping assistance for cu: Would you like mapping assistance for custom columns?
You can change it using the dropdown if: You can change it using the dropdown if needed.
You can change this at any time.
You deploy and maintain your own LibreWx: You deploy and maintain your own LibreWxR instance.

You deploy and maintain your own LibreWxR instance. It must be
              reachable by Caddy (your front-end host).
You have a dark-theme logo but no light-:
You have a dark-theme logo but no light-theme logo.
        Visitors using a light colour scheme will see your dark logo automatically
        colour-inverted, which may not look correct. Upload a light-theme logo above
        to control how it appears.
You have a light-theme logo but no dark-:
You have a light-theme logo but no dark-theme logo.
        Visitors using a dark colour scheme will see your light logo automatically
        colour-inverted, which may not look correct. Upload a dark-theme logo above
        to control how it appears.
You must check the acceptance box to con: You must check the acceptance box to continue. Please scroll through the Agreement and check the box.
You must scroll through and check this b:
You must scroll through and check this box to continue. Acceptance is recorded
        with a timestamp and stored in your wizard session.
Your dashboard will be available at the:
Your dashboard will be available at the address you configured.
      If you are accessing it on your local network, open a browser and enter the
      IP address or hostname of your weather server.
Your setup session has expired. Go back: Your setup session has expired. Go back to step 1 and reconnect to the API.
Your setup session has expired. Go back to step 1 to reconnect.
Your weather dashboard is being configur:
Your weather dashboard is being configured.

help.admin.analytics.body:
Configure visitor analytics tracking and privacy compliance settings for your dashboard.

### Google Analytics

Enter your Google Analytics Measurement ID (starts with `G-`) to enable visitor tracking. Leave blank to disable all analytics — no tracking code will be loaded.

### Privacy regions

Controls the consent banner shown to visitors:

- **Global** — Consent banner for all visitors
- **EU (GDPR)** — Consent banner for EU visitors
- **US (CCPA)** — Opt-out notice for California visitors
- **Both** — Applies EU and US rules together

The consent banner manages cookie consent for analytics. Even without analytics, selecting the correct region is good practice.
help.admin.analytics.tip: If you disable Google Analytics by clearing the ID, no tracking code will be loaded regardless of the privacy region setting.
help.admin.analytics.title: Analytics & Privacy Settings
help.admin.branding.body:
Customise how your weather dashboard looks and identifies itself to visitors.

- **Site Title** — Appears in the browser tab and dashboard header
- **Copyright Entity** — Shown in the footer
- **Accent Color** — Primary highlight color for buttons, links, and active states
- **Default Theme Mode** — Light, Dark, Auto (OS), or Auto (sunrise/sunset)
- **Logos** — Separate uploads for light and dark themes; provide alt text for accessibility
- **Favicon** — Browser tab icon (ICO or PNG, max 100 KB)
- **Custom CSS** — URL to a stylesheet that loads after default styles for advanced overrides

Changes are visible to dashboard visitors immediately after saving.
help.admin.branding.tip: Changes apply immediately. Visitors may need to refresh their browser to see updated logos or favicons due to caching.
help.admin.branding.title: Branding & Appearance
help.admin.card_layout.body:
Arrange the cards that appear on the Now page — the dashboard's home page showing current conditions.

### How it works

Cards are arranged in a grid. Drag cards to reorder them, or use the arrow buttons for precise placement. Move cards between **Active** (visible on the page) and **Available** (hidden from the page) zones.

### Card footprints

Some cards support different **footprints** (sizes). A larger footprint makes the card span more columns in the grid. Use the size selector on each card to choose the best fit.

### Saving

Click **Save Layout** to apply your changes. The new layout takes effect immediately for all dashboard visitors.
help.admin.card_layout.tip: Drag cards between Active and Available to show or hide them. The order in Active determines the display order on the page.
help.admin.card_layout.title: Now Page Card Layout
help.admin.column_mapping.body:
Review and update how your weewx database columns map to canonical field names used by the Clear Skies API.

### When to change mappings

Update mappings when you:

- Add a new sensor or weewx extension that creates new database columns
- Replace a sensor and the new one writes to a different column
- Want to include a previously-excluded column in the API

### Impact of changes

Changing a column mapping affects which data the API serves and how charts display. The change takes effect after the next API restart. Historical data for remapped columns will appear under the new field name.
help.admin.column_mapping.title: Column Mapping
help.admin.connection.body:
This section configures how the Config UI communicates with the Clear Skies API backend.

### API URL

The **API URL** is the internal address the Caddy reverse proxy uses to forward requests to the API. In a typical two-host setup, this is the weewx host's IP or hostname on port 8765 (e.g. `https://192.168.1.100:8765`).

Only change this if you have moved the API to a different server or changed its port.

### Bind host and port

The **bind host** and **bind port** are read-only here — they show where the API is currently listening. These are configured in `api.conf` on the weewx host and cannot be changed from the admin interface.

### Saving

Clicking **Save & Reload Caddy** updates the proxy configuration and reloads Caddy to route traffic to the new API address.
help.admin.connection.tip: Only change the API URL if you have moved the API to a different server. The default configuration rarely needs updating.
help.admin.connection.title: API Connection Settings
help.admin.earthquakes.body:
Configure the default display settings for the Seismic page.

- **Radius** — Search distance from your station (in kilometres). Earthquakes outside this radius are not shown.
- **Minimum Magnitude** — Only display earthquakes at or above this magnitude. The Richter scale is logarithmic — a magnitude 3.0 quake releases roughly 32 times more energy than a 2.0.
- **Default Time Range** — The initial time window shown to visitors. They can change this in the dashboard.

These are defaults — visitors can adjust the display filters on the Seismic page itself.
help.admin.earthquakes.title: Earthquake Display Settings
help.admin.forecast_correction.body:
The forecast correction engine improves your forecast provider's temperature predictions by learning from your station's actual observations.

### How it works

The engine collects **forecast-observation pairs** — comparing what the forecast predicted against what your station actually measured. After collecting enough pairs (at least 100), it trains a **Random Forest** machine learning model that learns systematic biases in the forecast.

Once trained, the model corrects future forecasts automatically before they reach the dashboard. For example, if your provider consistently predicts 2°F too warm in the afternoon, the correction engine learns and adjusts for this.

### TruScore metrics

After training, four metrics show the improvement:

- **Provider Score** — Accuracy of the raw forecast (100 = perfect)
- **Correction Score** — Accuracy after correction (should be higher)
- **MAE (raw)** — Average error of the raw forecast in degrees
- **MAE (corrected)** — Average error after correction (should be lower)

### Controls

- **Apply corrections** — Enable or disable applying corrections to forecasts
- **Collect pairs** — Enable or disable pair collection (turn off to freeze the training data)
- **Retrain Now** — Manually trigger model retraining. Normally this happens on schedule automatically.
help.admin.forecast_correction.tip: The engine needs at least 100 forecast-observation pairs before training. Collection runs automatically — just leave it enabled.
help.admin.forecast_correction.title: Forecast Correction Engine
help.admin.geographic_features.body:
Download geographic boundary data (borders, coastlines, labels) for the satellite and radar map overlay on the dashboard.

### What it provides

The geographic features data comes from OpenStreetMap and is stored in PMTiles format. It adds country and state borders, coastlines, city labels, and other geographic context to the radar and satellite views.

### Download

Click **Update Map Data** to download the latest geographic data. This is a one-time operation — the data does not change frequently. Only one download can run at a time.

### Status

The status indicator shows whether geographic data is available, its file size, and when it was last updated. If no data is downloaded, the satellite map will show weather imagery without geographic context.
help.admin.geographic_features.tip: Download once after initial setup. Update periodically (every few months) if you want the latest map data.
help.admin.geographic_features.title: Geographic Features
help.admin.haze_calibration.body:
The haze detection system identifies conditions where reduced visibility is caused by haze rather than clouds. It works by comparing your solar radiation readings against a nearby government reference air quality monitoring station.

### How calibration works

Clear Skies builds a **per-month clear-sky baseline** (the Kcs value) by collecting data from your sensor and the reference station over time. Each month needs its own baseline because atmospheric conditions vary seasonally.

Calibration status shows how many of the 12 months have sufficient data. Full calibration (all 12 months) provides the best accuracy, but partial calibration still improves results for the months that have data.

### Bootstrap sensor

The system automatically selects the nearest OpenAQ reference monitor. You can override this selection if you know of a closer or more representative monitor.

### Settings

- **Enable haze detection** — Turns the feature on or off
- **Gamma exponent** — Controls sensitivity to humidity. The default (0.45) works for most locations. Lower values reduce humidity sensitivity; higher values increase it.

### Reset

The **Reset Calibration** button deletes all per-month baselines and starts the calibration process over. Use this only if you have changed your solar radiation sensor or relocated your station.
help.admin.haze_calibration.tip: Calibration runs automatically in the background. Full calibration takes up to 12 months — one month of data per calendar month.
help.admin.haze_calibration.title: Haze Detection Calibration
help.admin.landing.body:
This is the admin dashboard for your Clear Skies installation. It shows a summary of your current configuration across all settings areas.

### Navigation

Use the **sidebar** on the left to jump directly to any section. Sections are grouped by topic: Station, Providers, Appearance, Dashboard, and Advanced.

### Making changes

Click **Edit** on any section card to open its editor. Changes are saved individually per section — saving one section does not affect others. Most changes take effect immediately after saving.

### Re-running the wizard

For major configuration changes (switching database hosts, changing the API connection), use the **Re-run Setup Wizard** link in the sidebar. The wizard preserves your existing settings as defaults.
help.admin.landing.tip: Click Edit on any section to update settings. Changes take effect immediately after saving.
help.admin.landing.title: Admin Overview
help.admin.pages.body:
Control which pages appear in the dashboard navigation menu. Hiding a page removes it from the menu and prevents visitors from accessing it directly.

The **Now** page is always visible and cannot be hidden — it is the dashboard's home page.

Hiding a page does **not** delete any data or configuration. You can show a hidden page at any time by unchecking it here.
help.admin.pages.tip: Use this to temporarily hide pages that are not yet ready, or permanently hide features you do not use.
help.admin.pages.title: Page Visibility
help.admin.sky_classification.body:
Clear Skies classifies sky conditions (Clear, Few Clouds, Scattered, Broken, Overcast) by comparing your solar radiation sensor readings to the theoretical maximum for your location and time.

### The Km clearness index

**Km** is the ratio of measured solar radiation to the maximum possible radiation (clear-sky model). A Km of 1.0 means perfectly clear; 0.0 means no sunlight reaching the sensor.

### Threshold meanings

- **Few Max** — Above this Km, sky is classified as Clear. Below it, Few Clouds.
- **Scattered Max** — Below Few Max but above this value: Scattered clouds.
- **Broken Max** — Below Scattered Max but above this: Broken clouds.
- **Overcast Km / Kv** — Below Broken Max and meeting these thresholds: Overcast.
- **SZA Minimum Elevation** — Sun must be at least this many degrees above the horizon for classification to run. Below this, conditions default to the provider's forecast.

### When to adjust

The default thresholds are calibrated for Davis Vantage Pro2 sensors. If your sensor consistently reports higher or lower values than expected, adjust the thresholds. The Kasten-Czeplak reference table below shows the relationship between Km values and standard okta-based sky cover descriptions.
help.admin.sky_classification.tip: The defaults work well for Davis Vantage Pro2 sensors. Only adjust if your sky classifications are consistently wrong.
help.admin.sky_classification.title: Sky Classification Thresholds
help.admin.social.body:
Add links to your social media profiles. These appear as icon links in the dashboard footer, giving visitors a way to follow your weather station on other platforms.

Enter the full URL for each profile (e.g. `https://facebook.com/yourpage`). Leave any field blank to hide that social link from the footer.

Supported platforms: Facebook, Twitter/X, Instagram, and YouTube.
help.admin.social.title: Social Media Links
help.admin.tls.body:
Manage how Caddy handles HTTPS certificates for your dashboard.

### Changing modes

You can switch TLS modes at any time. For example, you might start with self-signed certificates during setup and switch to ACME once your domain is pointed at your server.

### Certificate renewal

When using ACME (Let's Encrypt), certificates are renewed automatically by Caddy. No manual action is needed. Renewal typically happens 30 days before expiry.

### Mode descriptions

- **Self-signed** — Development only; browsers show a warning
- **ACME HTTP-01** — Automatic certs; requires port 80 access
- **ACME DNS-01** — Automatic certs via DNS; no port 80 needed
- **Manual** — Your own certificate and key files
- **Behind Proxy** — TLS handled by an upstream proxy
help.admin.tls.tip: ACME certificates renew automatically. Check the Caddy logs if you receive certificate expiry notices by email.
help.admin.tls.title: TLS / HTTPS Configuration
help.admin.webcam.body:
Enable or disable the webcam feature and configure the image and video URLs.

- **Enable Webcam** — When unchecked, the Webcam page is hidden from dashboard navigation
- **Still Image URL** — Path to the current snapshot image, updated by your webcam software
- **Timelapse Video URL** — Path to a timelapse video file
- **Refresh Interval** — How often (in seconds) the dashboard re-fetches the snapshot image

The URLs are relative to your web root unless they start with `http://` or `https://`.
help.admin.webcam.title: Webcam Settings
help.wizard.step_api.body:
The Clear Skies API is a program that runs on your **weewx host** — the same machine (or container) where weewx is installed. It reads your weather station data from the weewx database and makes it available to your weather website (the **dashboard** — the public page your visitors see).

This wizard needs to connect to the API so it can configure your station on your behalf.

### First-time setup

When the API starts for the first time, it displays a startup banner containing the **trust token** and **certificate fingerprint** you need to enter here.

**Where to find the banner:**

- If you started the API manually in a terminal, the banner appears right in that terminal window.
- If the API is running as a system service, view the banner with:
  `sudo journalctl -u weewx-clearskies-api --no-pager | head -20`

The banner looks like this:

```
No configuration found — starting in setup mode.
  Address:     https://your-host:8765
  Trust token: (your token here)
  Fingerprint: (your fingerprint here)
```

- The **trust token** is a single-use secret that proves you have access to the machine running the API. It can only be used once — if you need a new one, restart the API.
- The **certificate fingerprint** lets the wizard verify it is talking to *your* API and not someone else's. Compare the value shown in the wizard to the one in the startup banner — they should match.

### Re-running the wizard

If you have already completed setup and are re-running the wizard, the trust token is not needed — the connection is already established. Just verify the API address and click **Reconnect**.
help.wizard.step_api.tip: The default API port is 8765. Only change it if you configured a custom port during API installation.
help.wizard.step_api.title: Connecting to the Clear Skies API
help.wizard.step_appearance.body:
Customize how your weather site looks and identifies itself to visitors.

### Site identity

- **Site Title** appears in the browser tab and site header
- **Copyright Entity** appears in the footer (e.g. your name or organization)

### Logos

Upload separate logos for light and dark themes, or provide just one — it will be used for both. For each logo, you can either **upload a file** from your computer or **enter a path or URL** (e.g. `/images/my-logo.svg` for a file already on your server, or `https://example.com/logo.png` for an external image).

**File requirements:**
- Formats: **SVG** (preferred — scales to any size) or **PNG** with a transparent background
- Maximum file size: 500 KB
- Recommended dimensions: **wide/horizontal** layout works best — roughly 200–400 px wide by 40–80 px tall. A vertical or square logo will look oversized in the header.
- Use a **transparent background** so the logo blends with both light and dark themes

Provide **alt text** for each logo — a short description like "My Weather Station logo" that screen readers read aloud for visually impaired visitors (see the Station Photo section in step 6 for more on alt text).

### Favicon

The **favicon** is the small icon in the browser tab next to your site title. You can upload a file or enter a path/URL.

- Formats: **ICO** or **PNG**
- Recommended size: 32×32 px or 64×64 px
- Maximum file size: 100 KB
- Leave blank to use the default Clear Skies icon

### Colors and theme

- **Accent Color** sets the primary highlight color across your site (buttons, links, active indicators)
- **Default Theme Mode** controls the initial appearance:
  - *Light* or *Dark* — fixed theme
  - *Auto (OS)* — follows the visitor's system setting
  - *Auto (sunrise/sunset)* — switches based on your station's local daylight
help.wizard.step_appearance.tip: SVG logos look best because they stay sharp at any size. If using PNG, make sure the background is transparent.
help.wizard.step_appearance.title: Appearance & Branding
help.wizard.step_complete.body:
Your Clear Skies installation is configured and the API is restarting with your new settings.

### What to check

- **Dashboard** — Visit your dashboard URL in a browser. Current conditions should appear within a few minutes once the API cache warm completes.
- **API restart status** — The status indicator on this page updates automatically. Wait for it to show "running" before troubleshooting.
- **Provider data** — Forecast, alerts, and AQI data may take a few minutes to appear after the first startup.

### Next steps

- **Admin interface** — Use the admin panel to make changes after initial setup. You can update providers, adjust branding, rearrange the Now page layout, and more — all without re-running the wizard.
- **Haze calibration** — The haze detection system will begin collecting baseline data automatically. Full calibration takes up to 12 months.
- **Forecast correction** — If enabled, the forecast correction engine will begin collecting observation pairs. Training starts after enough data is collected.

### If something is not working

Use the troubleshooting section below for manual restart commands and common diagnostic steps. Check that the API service is running and that your firewall allows traffic on the required ports.
help.wizard.step_complete.tip: Bookmark the admin URL — you will use it to update settings, manage providers, and adjust the dashboard layout.
help.wizard.step_complete.title: Setup Complete
help.wizard.step_db.body:
Clear Skies reads weather observations from your weewx database. This step configures the connection to that database.

### How it works

weewx continuously writes weather observations to a database (the "archive"). Clear Skies reads from this same database to display current conditions, historical charts, and records on your dashboard. Clear Skies never writes to the weewx database — it is read-only.

### Detected database type

The wizard reads your `weewx.conf` to detect whether weewx is using **SQLite** (a single database file) or **MySQL/MariaDB** (a database server), and shows only the fields relevant to the detected type. The detected type is shown at the top of this step.

### Pre-filled values

If the API can read your `weewx.conf` file, the connection details (host/port/user/name for MySQL, or the file path for SQLite) are pre-filled automatically. Review them and adjust only if needed.

### Testing the connection

Click **Test Connection** to verify that the API can reach your database with the details entered. For MySQL/MariaDB, a successful test shows the database server version. For SQLite, a successful test confirms the file exists and is readable. If the test fails, double-check the values below.

### If you've forgotten your MySQL/MariaDB password

Check your `weewx.conf` under `[DatabaseTypes] [[MySQL]]`, or run `mysql -u root -p` to reset it.

### SQLite file path

The path should match what is configured in `weewx.conf`. The default weewx SQLite database is usually at `/var/lib/weewx/weewx.sdb`.

### Security

The database password is stored in `secrets.env` (file mode 0600), never in plain-text configuration files. Only the API process can read it.
help.wizard.step_db.tip: If your database is on the same machine as the API, use "localhost" as the host. This is the most common setup for MySQL/MariaDB. SQLite databases are always local files, so no host is needed.
help.wizard.step_db.title: Database Connection
help.wizard.step_eula.body:
This is the Clear Skies Operator License Agreement (EULA). It outlines the terms under which you may use the software.

### Key points

- Clear Skies is licensed under the **PolyForm Noncommercial License 1.0.0** for personal, educational, and nonprofit use.
- **Free use** includes: personal weather stations, community weather sharing (neighborhood groups, HOAs), family farms, amateur radio weather stations, citizen science networks, and tax-exempt nonprofits.
- **Commercial use requires a separate license.** This includes displaying advertising, offering paid subscriptions, managed hosting for third parties, and revenue-generating marketing activities.
- You are responsible for complying with the terms of service of any external data providers you configure (weather APIs, AQI services, etc.).

### Translated versions

If the wizard is set to a non-English language, you will see a translated version of the EULA provided for your convenience. **The English version is the sole legally binding document.** A link above the text lets you switch to the English version at any time.

### What acceptance means

By checking the box below, you confirm that you have read and agree to the EULA terms. Your acceptance is recorded with a timestamp. If the EULA is updated in a future version, you will be asked to review and accept the new terms before continuing.

The full license details are available in the **LICENSE** and **ADDITIONAL-USES.md** files in your installation directory.
help.wizard.step_eula.tip: Scroll through the full agreement text before the acceptance checkbox becomes available.
help.wizard.step_eula.title: Operator License Agreement
help.wizard.step_feature_settings.body:
Configure optional features that enhance your weather site.

### Seismic page

The Seismic page shows recent earthquakes near your station on an interactive map. These settings control what appears:

- **Radius** — How far from your station (in kilometers) to search for earthquakes. Larger values show more distant quakes.
- **Minimum Magnitude** — Only show earthquakes at or above this value on the **Moment Magnitude scale (Mw)**, which is the standard used by the USGS and other seismological agencies worldwide. It replaced the older Richter scale. The scale is logarithmic — each whole number represents roughly 32 times more energy released. For reference: magnitude 2.0 is rarely felt, 4.0 may be felt indoors, and 6.0+ can cause damage.
- **Default Time Range** — How many days of earthquake history to show when a visitor first opens the Seismic page (for example, "30 days" shows the past month of activity). Visitors can change this on the page.

These settings are a starting point — visitors can adjust the display on the Seismic page itself. If no earthquake provider is configured (see the Providers step), the Seismic page will not appear.
help.wizard.step_feature_settings.tip: A radius of 250 km with a minimum magnitude of 2.0 is a good starting point for most locations.
help.wizard.step_feature_settings.title: Feature Settings
help.wizard.step_import.body:
If you are migrating from an existing weewx skin (such as Belchertown), Clear Skies can read your old skin's configuration file and pre-fill many of the settings in the steps that follow.

### What gets imported

- Station location (latitude, longitude, altitude)
- Display units (temperature, speed, pressure, rain)
- Some provider settings (forecast source, radar configuration)
- Station name and description

### Chart layouts

Belchertown charts live in a separate `graphs.conf` file. Upload it in the **Chart Layout Migration** section below and Clear Skies will automatically convert it to the Clear Skies chart format. This is optional — you can configure charts manually later from the admin interface if you skip it.

### What does not transfer automatically

- API keys and passwords (you will re-enter these in the Providers step)
- Custom CSS or JavaScript

### Starting fresh

If you do not have an existing skin or prefer to configure everything from scratch, click **Skip — Start Fresh**. The wizard will use sensible US-unit defaults as a starting point. Every setting can be adjusted in the steps that follow.
help.wizard.step_import.tip: The default skin name "Belchertown" matches the standard installation directory. Change it only if your skin is installed under a different name.
help.wizard.step_import.title: Importing an Existing Configuration
help.wizard.step_language.body:
This step sets the language for the **setup wizard and admin interface** — the screens you, the operator, see while configuring Clear Skies.

It does **not** set the language your dashboard visitors see. That is configured later in the **Station Identity** step as the "Default language" setting. Visitors can also override the dashboard language in their own browser.

Clear Skies supports 13 languages. Click a language card to switch immediately. You can change the wizard language at any time by clicking the language selector in the sidebar.
help.wizard.step_language.tip: Your choice is saved in a browser cookie. If you clear cookies, the wizard will revert to English.
help.wizard.step_language.title: Choosing Your Language
help.wizard.step_privacy_legal.body:
This step configures visitor tracking, privacy compliance, and the legal pages on your weather site.

### Visitor analytics

Visitor analytics lets you see how many people visit your weather site, which pages they view, and where they come from. Clear Skies supports **Google Analytics**. If you have a Google Analytics account, enter your Measurement ID (it starts with `G-`). If you leave this blank, no tracking code is loaded and no visitor data is collected.

### Privacy regions

Privacy laws in some regions require you to show a **consent banner** — a notice that asks visitors for permission before loading tracking scripts like Google Analytics.

- **Global** — shows a consent banner to all visitors regardless of location
- **EU (GDPR)** — shows a consent banner to visitors from the European Union, as required by European data protection law
- **US (CCPA)** — shows opt-out options for California visitors under the California Consumer Privacy Act
- **Both (EU + US)** — applies both GDPR and CCPA rules

**You are responsible for choosing the correct setting for your situation and complying with the privacy laws that apply to your visitors.** Clear Skies provides the consent banner mechanism, but we cannot determine which laws apply to your site — that depends on where your visitors are located and where you operate. When in doubt, **Global** is the safest choice.

### Legal pages

Your weather site includes a Legal page with built-in **Terms of Use** and **Privacy Policy** templates. These are basic documents provided as a starting point for your convenience.

**Important:** These templates are **not legal advice**. We are not lawyers. You are responsible for verifying that these documents are adequate and legally valid in your jurisdiction. If they are not, you should upload your own versions.

The built-in legal templates are available in all 13 supported languages. If you upload your own replacement documents, they will be shown in the language you upload them in — Clear Skies does not translate uploaded documents. If your visitors speak a language other than the one you upload, you will need to provide your own translations.

To upload custom documents, use the file upload fields below. Accepted formats: Markdown (`.md`) or plain text (`.txt`). Leave blank to use the built-in templates. You can revert to the defaults at any time from the admin interface.
help.wizard.step_privacy_legal.tip: When in doubt, select Global for the broadest privacy coverage. If you do not use Google Analytics, the consent banner has no practical effect but does no harm.
help.wizard.step_privacy_legal.title: Privacy, Legal & Analytics
help.wizard.step_providers.body:
Clear Skies fetches supplemental data from external providers to enrich your weather site beyond what your station measures directly. This step lets you choose a provider for each data type and enter any required API keys.

### Data types

- **Forecast** — Multi-day weather forecasts (temperature, conditions, precipitation probability)
- **Weather Alerts** — Severe weather warnings and advisories for your area
- **Air Quality (AQI)** — Air quality index and pollutant concentrations. Some providers report **observed** values (from nearby monitoring stations) while others use **model-based** estimates (calculated from satellite and weather data)
- **Earthquakes** — Recent seismic activity near your station
- **Radar** — Weather radar imagery overlays

### Choosing a provider

For each data type, select the provider that best fits your needs. Some providers are free with no signup required (marked **"no key needed"**), while others require an API key that you get from the provider's website.

### API keys

Most providers offer free tiers that are sufficient for a personal weather station. Keys are entered inline and stored securely in `secrets.env` on the server. Use the **Test** button to verify each key works before continuing.

### AQI Regional Scale

Some AQI providers support multiple regional scales (US EPA, European, Chinese, etc.). If this option appears, choose the scale most familiar to your visitors.
help.wizard.step_providers.tip: You can change providers later in the admin interface without re-running the wizard.
help.wizard.step_providers.title: Data Providers
help.wizard.step_review.body:
Review your complete configuration before applying it. Each section shows a summary of the values you entered.

### Making changes

Click the **Edit** link on any section to jump back to that step and make changes. When you return, the review page will show your updated values.

### What happens when you click Apply

Applying your configuration does several things:

1. Sends the API configuration (database, providers, units, column mapping) to the Clear Skies API on your weewx host
2. Writes `branding.json` (visual settings), `stack.conf` (local config), and `secrets.env` (passwords and API keys) to the local configuration directory
3. Restarts the API service to load the new configuration

### After applying

The API takes approximately **2 minutes** to restart while it warms its data caches (fetching forecasts, AQI data, etc.). During this time the dashboard may show incomplete data. The next page will show the restart status.
help.wizard.step_review.tip: Double-check your API keys and database password before applying — masked values show only the first and last few characters.
help.wizard.step_review.title: Review & Apply
help.wizard.step_schema.body:
Your weewx database has columns for each type of weather measurement — temperature, humidity, wind speed, and so on. This step tells Clear Skies what each column contains so it can display that data on your dashboard.

**If a column is not mapped, Clear Skies cannot read it.** That measurement will not appear anywhere on your weather site — no current reading, no charts, no historical records. The data is still safe in your weewx database, but Clear Skies will not know it exists.

### Stock columns

Standard weewx columns (like `outTemp`, `outHumidity`, `windSpeed`) are recognised automatically and mapped for you. These appear in the **Stock columns** table and require no action.

### Custom columns

If your station has extra sensors or weewx extensions that add non-standard columns, they appear in the **Custom columns** table. For each one, use the dropdown to **tell Clear Skies what the column measures** (for example, "soil temperature" or "UV index"). If you are not sure what a column contains, you can skip it and map it later from the admin interface.

### Confidence levels

Clear Skies tries to guess what each custom column contains based on its name and the data values it finds:

- **High** — very confident match (the column name closely matches a known measurement)
- **Medium** — likely match, worth a quick review
- **Low / Suggested** — best guess, review carefully before accepting

### Unit detection

Clear Skies detects the unit for each column from your weewx configuration. The source is shown as a badge: **weewx** (from your config) or **heuristic** (inferred from data values). You can override detected units if they are incorrect.
help.wizard.step_schema.tip: If you skip a custom column now, you can always map it later from the admin interface — no data is lost from your weewx database.
help.wizard.step_schema.title: Column Mapping
help.wizard.step_station.body:
This step defines who and where your weather station is. The information entered here appears throughout your weather site and is used for calculations behind the scenes.

### Pre-filled values

If the API was able to read your `weewx.conf` file, these fields are already filled in: station name, latitude, longitude, altitude, and timezone. Review them and adjust if needed — the values come from your weewx configuration and may be out of date or approximate.

**Important:** Clear Skies does not write changes back to `weewx.conf`. If you correct your latitude, altitude, or other values here, you should also update your `weewx.conf` file to keep them in sync.

### Location

**Latitude and longitude** are used for:

- Sun and moon position calculations (sunrise, sunset, solar noon)
- Automatic provider selection (matching providers to your region)
- Automatic timezone detection
- Sky condition classification (solar elevation angles)

Enter coordinates in **decimal degrees** — for example, `40.7128` for latitude and `-74.0060` for longitude (New York City). Use the **Use my location** button if your weather station is near your current device.

### Altitude

Station altitude is used for barometric pressure calculations. Enter your station's elevation above sea level. If unknown, enter 0 — pressure readings may be slightly less accurate.

### Timezone

The timezone is auto-detected from your coordinates. Override it only if the detection is incorrect. This controls how timestamps are displayed throughout your weather site.

### Default language

This sets the language your **website visitors** see by default when they open your weather site. It is pre-filled from the language you chose at the start of this wizard, since most operators want their visitors to see the same language. You can change it here if your visitors speak a different language than you do.

Visitors can always switch to a different language in their browser — this setting just controls the default.

### Station photo

You can upload a photo of your weather station — this appears on the About page of your weather site. The photo must be under 2 MB.

When you upload a photo, you will be asked for **alt text** — this is a short description of what the photo shows (for example, "Davis Vantage Pro2 mounted on a rooftop pole"). Alt text is read aloud by screen readers for visitors who are blind or visually impaired, and it appears in place of the image if the photo fails to load.

### Station description

The description field lets you add a few sentences about your station — the equipment you use, where it is located, when you started recording, or anything else visitors might find interesting. This text appears on the About page below your station photo. Keep it to 2–3 sentences, as longer text can crowd the page layout.
help.wizard.step_station.tip: The most important fields are latitude, longitude, and timezone — these affect sun calculations and provider selection.
help.wizard.step_station.title: Station Identity
help.wizard.step_tls.body:
### What is TLS?

**TLS** (Transport Layer Security) is what makes the padlock icon appear in your browser's address bar. It encrypts the connection between your visitors' browsers and your server so no one can intercept the data in transit. You may also see it called **HTTPS** or **SSL** — they all refer to the same thing.

Without TLS, browsers may flag your site as "Not Secure," which can discourage visitors from trusting your weather data.

### Choosing a mode

- **Self-signed (development)** — You provide your own self-signed certificate. Clear Skies does not generate one for you. Browsers will show a security warning to every visitor because the certificate is not issued by a trusted authority. Only use this for testing or private networks where you control all the devices. If your self-signed certificate is on a separate proxy (not installed on this server), select **Behind Proxy** instead.

- **ACME HTTP-01 (Let's Encrypt)** — Clear Skies automatically obtains and renews a free, publicly trusted certificate from Let's Encrypt. **Choose this only if this server is directly exposed to the internet** on port 80 — Let's Encrypt needs to reach your server to verify you own the domain. Enter your domain name and an email address for certificate expiry notices. If you are running ACME on a separate tool like Nginx Proxy Manager (NPM), Traefik, or another reverse proxy, select **Behind Proxy** instead.

- **ACME DNS-01 (Let's Encrypt, DNS)** — Same free certificates as HTTP-01, but validates domain ownership via DNS records instead of port 80. Use this if port 80 is blocked by your ISP or firewall, or if you need a wildcard certificate (`*.yourdomain.com`). Requires a DNS provider API token.

- **Manual** — Provide your own certificate and private key files. Use this if you have certificates from a corporate certificate authority (CA), a purchased certificate, or another source. Files must be in **PEM format**. The certificate file should contain your server certificate followed by any intermediate certificates (the full chain), in that order. The key file contains the corresponding private key.

- **Behind Proxy** — Choose this if something else sits in front of Clear Skies and handles the TLS encryption for you. Common examples:
  - **Nginx Proxy Manager (NPM)** or **Traefik** handling ACME/Let's Encrypt
  - **Cloudflare** or another CDN providing TLS termination
  - **A corporate load balancer** that terminates SSL
  - A separate server running a self-signed certificate

  In this mode, Clear Skies accepts plain (unencrypted) HTTP from the upstream proxy and does not manage certificates at all.
help.wizard.step_tls.tip: For a public weather site, use a publicly trusted certificate (ACME or Manual with a real CA). Self-signed certificates cause browser security warnings that will discourage most visitors.
help.wizard.step_tls.title: TLS / HTTPS Configuration
help.wizard.step_units.body:
Choose how weather measurements are displayed on your weather site. This affects what your visitors see — it does not change how weewx stores data internally.

### Presets

Three presets fill all fields at once:

- **US** — Fahrenheit, mph, inHg, inches of rain
- **Metric** — Celsius, km/h, mbar, centimeters of rain
- **Metric (WX)** — Celsius, m/s, mbar, millimeters of rain (the standard used in professional meteorology)

### Per-group customization

After selecting a preset, you can override individual measurement groups. For example, you might prefer US units overall but want wind speed in km/h.

### Visitor experience

The units you set here are the **defaults** shown to visitors on your weather site. Visitors can switch units in the interface if they prefer different display units.
help.wizard.step_units.title: Display Units
help.wizard.step_webcam.body:
If you have a webcam pointed at your weather station or surrounding area, Clear Skies can display a live still image and an optional timelapse video on the Webcam page of your weather site.

### How it works

Your webcam software (such as motionEye, ZoneMinder, or a simple cron job) saves a snapshot image to a URL at regular intervals. Clear Skies loads that image from the URL you provide and displays it to your visitors.

### Supported formats

- **Still images:** JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP
- **Timelapse video:** MP4 (H.264). Other video formats are not supported.

### Configuration

- **Still Image URL** — Where to find the current snapshot. You can use a relative path on the same server (e.g. `/webcam/weather_cam.jpg`) or a full URL to an external host (e.g. `https://cam.example.com/latest.jpg`). The default assumes your webcam software saves to a `webcam/` directory under the web root.
- **Timelapse Video URL** — Where to find the timelapse video. Same format as above — a relative path (e.g. `/webcam/weewx_timelapse.mp4`) or a full URL. Must be an MP4 file.
- **Refresh Interval** — How often (in seconds) the page re-fetches the still image. For example, `60` means the image updates once a minute. Lower values show more current images but increase bandwidth.

### Disabling the webcam

If you do not have a webcam, leave the **Enable Webcam** checkbox unchecked. The Webcam page will not appear in your site's navigation.
help.wizard.step_webcam.tip: The default paths work with most webcam setups that save to a standard web directory. A refresh interval of 60 seconds is a good starting point.
help.wizard.step_webcam.title: Webcam Setup
